title: Eriolepis laniflora
intro: Eriophyton laniflorum, commonly known as Woolly Leaf, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae that is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriophyton laniflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Woolly Leaf include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to relieve respiratory issues and alleviate fever.
